item.title: Finance Minister to visit China on trade-diversification mission
item.description: Champagne will meet senior Chinese finance and banking officials during a visit from April 1 to 4

item.title: Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec
item.description: The post Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Alto’s high-speed rail project Toronto to Quebec City sparks backlash over cost, land use
item.description: Eastern Ontario residents and local leaders warn the multibillion-dollar project threatens communities while offering few benefits to those footing the bill

item.title: A major immigration reform bill is now law in Canada. Some worry it rolls back refugee rights
item.description: After a week that saw a scathing auditor general's report find the federal Immigration Department has failed to act on fraud in its international student program, a major bill reforming immigration powers is now law in Canada.

item.title: 'Light also exists': Some Cubans see hope in the darkness of the moment
item.description: The U.S. oil blockade has pushed Cuba into a third month of energy crisis, deepening daily hardship in a country already strained by crumbling infrastructure. But amid the darkness, some Cubans still see hope for a better future.

item.title: She had a family and a business in Mississauga’s Afghan community. How did Mezhgan Aini vanish and no one reported it for three years?
Canadian banks, pension funds have poured billions into ICE contractors
item.description: OTTAWA - Major Canadian banks and pension funds have provided tens of billions of dollars to American contractors for the U.S. Immigration and…

item.title: New NDP Leader Avi Lewis faces a complex political dynamic in rebuilding party
item.description: Provincial counterparts had mixed reactions to his decisive first-ballot victory

item.title: Avi Lewis is the new NDP leader, as Alberta, Sask. leaders say his polices can ‘hurt’
Prime Minister Carney plans to attend Sunday’s Juno Awards
Pro-Palestine activists wanted to test the limits of a residential protest ban in North York. Here’s what happened
Avi Lewis elected NDP leader after successful populist, left-wing campaign
Bomb threat at Four Seasons Toronto forces evacuation, causes Shen Yun to cancel on second night
NDP leadership vote recap: Avi Lewis talks Canada’s role in Iran, wealth tax and ‘care economy,’ takes flak from NDP leaders in the Prairies
Nearly 82,000 potholes repaired in Toronto after third blitz in the city
item.description: The city said it was receiving pothole hotspot reports from residents, and that crews usually respond to reports within four days.

item.title: Althia Raj: Avi Lewis inherited a divided and weakened NDP. Here’s how he plans to turn it around
Lewis’ election as NDP leader divides party’s Western base
item.description: While the election of Avi Lewis as the new federal NDP leader has received mixed response, Lewis says he'll focus on strengthening the grassroots before seeking elected office.

item.title: Provincial supervisor announces first round of cuts at Toronto Catholic school board. Here’s what is targeted
The NDP has a new leader. What does Avi Lewis's arrival mean for the party?
item.description: Newly named NDP leader Avi Lewis promised to bring back the party from the political wilderness after its dismal showing in last year's federal election.

item.title: European Parliament member says she was denied entry to Canada due to pro-Palestinian views
item.description: Rima Hassan claims she was ‘prevented’ from entering the country, describing it as an attempt at censorship

item.title: Avi Lewis vows ‘NDP comeback starts now’ after winning leadership race
item.description: Avi Lewis received more than 50 per cent of the total votes cast during the NDP leadership convention, giving him the victory on the first ballot.

item.title: Jewish advocacy group presses Ottawa to boost community security spending
item.description: Centre for Israel and Jewish Affairs wrote to finance minister ahead of spring economic statement after report warned of possible extremist attack

item.title: Carney government sidesteps questions on forced labour in China
Avi Lewis wins NDP leadership race in decisive first-ballot victory
Avi Lewis previews his leadership vision as New Democrats express mix of unease, excitement
item.description: With all eyes on front-runner Lewis, NDP leadership contenders made their last pitches to delegates Saturday. Voting results will be announced Sunday.

item.title: As prime minister, Mark Carney makes Question Period a low priority
item.description: A Global News analysis of Hansard shows Prime Minister Mark Carney has attended Question Period less than 30 per cent of the time, much less than either of his two predecessors.

item.title: Tens of thousands are expected to live on Toronto’s booming waterfront. Jets at Billy Bishop may change all that
Winning matters, Manitoba premier warns federal New Democrats
Ottawa's big bet on world's largest cricket farm ran into a simple problem: the 'yuck factor'
item.description: Ottawa backed the world's largest cricket farm with tens of millions in public money. It collapsed before reaching full production, exposing a gap between hype and demand while also leaving questions about how much of that money was ever recovered.

item.title: Energy policy key as NDP leadership candidates make final pitch
item.description: WINNIPEG - The NDP leadership candidates made their last pitch to voters Saturday morning in a last-ditch effort to win support from the handf…

item.title: China denies forced labour allegations amid fallout from Michael Ma’s comments
Ontario Premier Doug Ford heading to Texas to talk trade and tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford will push a pro-trade and anti-tariff message during a multi-day trip to Texas, U.S. where he will meet Governor Greg Abbott, an ally of President Donald Trump, and business leaders.

item.title: Nate Erskine-Smith urges Liberal rivals to step aside in Scarborough Southwest byelection
item.description: The MP for Beaches—East York knows losing the byelection nomination could derail his campaign to be the next leader of the Ontario Liberals.

item.title: Feminists urge continuing fight of slain Iraqi-Canadian, Yanar Mohammed
Ontario’s budget banks on a housing resale rebound in 2026 — but real estate players aren’t so sure
2026 FIFA World Cup will make Toronto even busier. Here’s how the city plans to keep people moving
NDP leadership candidates to make their final pitch to voters at Winnipeg convention
item.description: The NDP leadership candidates are making their last pitch to party members before voting in the six-month campaign concludes later today.

item.title: Mark Carney’s strategy for the public service becomes clearer
item.description: It’s not a public-service overhaul, it’s a workaround

item.title: Bill C-12 curbing asylum, immigration rights becomes law amid warnings of court challenges
item.description: Law fast-tracks deportation for some asylum seekers and allows Ottawa to share immigration information with provinces

item.title: With finish line in sight, NDP leadership candidates try to win over voters
item.description: The five candidates vying for the NDP's leadership into the next election gave one final appeal to members before the party chooses its next leader.

item.title: Federal New Democrats are in Manitoba — but do Prairie NDP leaders want them there?
item.description: Despite Premier Wab Kinew delivering words of affirmation to New Democrats gathered in Winnipeg, sources says the Manitoba NDP has tried to distance itself from the federal wing of the party, as tensions with Prairie New Democrats are once again playing out in the leadership race.

item.title: Should gambling taxes fund sports? Former Olympian says it's a 'devil's bargain'
item.description: The government will be making a "devil's bargain" if it moves ahead with a recommendation to boost revenue for sport organizations through taxes on sports gambling, former track and field Olympian Bruce Kidd said.

item.title: Canadian Army overhaul looms as DND finalizes mobilization plans
item.description: Canada's army is preparing a sweeping reorganization just as the National Defence Department moves to finalize its mobilization plan. Senior commanders warn the current force is ill suited to a more dangerous world, with proposals under study to expand reserves and generate hundreds of thousands of additional personnel in a crisis.

item.title: What happens after communism? Cubans face huge challenges, big decisions
item.description: Cuba appears to be tottering toward some kind of political transition. Some Cubans are concerned that the residents own nothing and are too impoverished to take over the few functioning businesses on the island, while there is a large and wealthy diaspora community (and potentially also foreign investors) who would be able to swoop in and buy property and businesses at bargain prices.

item.title: Conservatives put pressure on Carney to clarify his position on forced labour in China
item.description: Move follows comments by Liberal MP Michael Ma that appeared to cast doubt that the practice takes place

item.title: Métis National Council president says RCMP statement of regret falls short
item.description: The president of the Métis National Council says the RCMP's response to an extensive spying program that targeted hundreds of Indigenous people falls short.

item.title: Politics Insider: Ottawa opens public sector buyout applications
item.description: And, the federal NDP leadership race heads to the finish line

item.title: Opposition fears Doug Ford's omnibus budget bill will be fast-tracked through to law
Canada will cancel thousands of refugee claims under new retroactive law
Sunshine List of $100,000 earners in public sector tops 400,000 for first time
Air Canada CEO's lack of respect outweighs his lack of French, crisis management expert says
item.description: The CEO of Canada’s largest airline remains under fire from multiple fronts this week following a botched expression of condolence and a subsequent fumbled apology.

item.title: 'This is a dumb war,' Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew says in letter urging PM to take action to stop Iran conflict
item.description: Manitoba's premier is calling on Mark Carney to help stop the war in Iran.

item.title: Toronto Humber Yacht Club shut down by council — even as club members vow ‘it’s not over’
item.description: Council voted Thursday to end the club's 70-year-long tenancy.

item.title: Today at Toronto city council: City to pay residents to shovel snow, orders Humber Yacht Club to close
item.description: The Star's city hall team will be updating throughout the council session that is scheduled to run Wednesday through Friday.

item.title: NDP says goodbye to the past, ponders what the future holds
item.description: New Democrats gave tribute to NDP former leader Jagmeet Singh on Friday — just days before they're set to announce his successor.

item.title: Israel pushes back against Canada’s criticism of its occupation of southern Lebanon
item.description: The IDF has to intervene because the Lebanese government can’t resolve the threat posed by Hezbollah, Israel’s deputy foreign minister says

item.title: George Brown college files ‘mass termination’ notice in face of steep drop in enrolment
item.description: The Toronto college has indicated it is laying off 22 hourly and 29 salaried employees amid budget pressures.

item.title: A sign Toronto’s election is unofficially underway? Attack ads target ‘failed mayor’ Olivia Chow
Outlook for Ontario housing starts weakens further away from 1.5 million goal
item.description: Ontario’s housing start projections have been revised downward once again in the province’s budget, putting the government even farther off track from building 1.5 million homes over 10 years — a target to which the ministers in charge say they pay no mind.

item.title: Pierre Poilievre backs J.K. Rowling’s support for new Olympic gender policy
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is supporting a ruling this week by the International Olympic Committee that bans transgender women from women's sports at the Games.

item.title: Liberal MP's Chinese forced labour comments come as U.S. probes Canada's imports
item.description: Liberal MP Michael Ma's apology for confronting an academic witness about whether her evidence on Chinese aluminum supply chains could be "hearsay" coincides with a highly sensitive moment in Canada's trade war with the Trump administration. The U.S. included Canada in its recent tariff investigation to crack down on countries suspected of turning a blind eye to forced labour.

item.title: International visitors helped boost Canada’s tourism industry in 2025: data
item.description: The agency said Friday that spending by international tourists grew 3.6 per cent in the fourth quarter of 2025 from the previous quarter, which was the fastest pace in two years.

item.title: Ottawa opens applications for public-sector buyouts, sets July 24 deadline
item.description: Letters went out in December to around 68,000 workers informing them that they may be eligible for the early retirement incentive

item.title: Canada launches inquiry on frozen and canned vegetable imports
‘We’re fixing it’: Toronto unveils new congestion plan as gridlock continues to mount
item.description: Video

item.title: Doug Ford is taking his anti-tariff campaign to Texas
Alberta referendum ‘can have an effect’ on investor confidence, Carney says
item.description: When asked whether he thought an Alberta referendum could impact investor confidence, Carney said, 'It can, yeah. It can have an effect.'

item.title: Doug Ford’s Tories table record $244B Ontario budget amid hefty deficit
10 things to know about Ontario’s budget
